perturbation.original_text: Customer shall pay the Monthly Base Fee in advance on the first day of the calendar month in which the Services are to be performed. Any and all other amounts payable under this Agreement shall be due thirty (30) days following the date of invoice, unless otherwise provided in the Services and Charges Schedule. Undisputed charges not paid by the applicable due date shall be subject to annual interest at the prevailing U.S. prime rate published by Citibank, N.A., from time to time or the highest rate permitted by law, whichever is lower. Customer shall also pay any collection fees, court costs, reasonable attorneys' fees, and other fees, costs, and charges incurred by Metavante in collecting payment of the charges and any other amounts for which Customer is liable under the terms and conditions of this Agreement and which shall be due thirty (30) days following the date of invoice. Customer agrees to maintain a depository account with Oriental Bank and Trust for the payment of amounts payable hereunder and hereby authorizes Metavante to initiate debit entries to such account for the payment of amounts payable hereunder. Customer agrees to provide Metavante with any and all information necessary for Metavante to initiate such debit entries via the Automated Clearing House (ACH) system.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer shall pay the Monthly Base Fee in advance on the first day of the calendar month in which the Services are to be performed. Any and all other amounts payable under this Agreement shall be due fifteen (15) days following the date of invoice, unless otherwise provided in the Services and Charges Schedule. Undisputed charges not paid by the applicable due date shall be subject to annual interest at the prevailing U.S. prime rate published by Citibank, N.A., from time to time or the highest rate permitted by law, whichever is lower. Customer shall also pay any collection fees, court costs, reasonable attorneys' fees, and other fees, costs, and charges incurred by Metavante in collecting payment of the charges and any other amounts for which Customer is liable under the terms and conditions of this Agreement and which shall be due thirty (30) days following the date of invoice. Customer agrees to maintain a depository account with Oriental Bank and Trust for the payment of amounts payable hereunder and hereby authorizes Metavante to initiate debit entries to such account for the payment of amounts payable hereunder. Customer agrees to provide Metavante with any and all information necessary for Metavante to initiate such debit entries via the Automated Clearing House (ACH) system.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 5.5 initially states that 'any and all other amounts payable under this agreement shall be due thirty (30) days following the date of invoice' but it has been modified to 'fifteen (15) days following the date of invoice'. The two different due dates creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5.5, causing ambiguity and potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer shall pay the Monthly Base Fee in advance on the first day of the calendar month in which the Services are to be performed. Any and all other amounts payable under this Agreement shall be due fifteen (15) days following the date of invoice, unless otherwise provided in the Services and Charges Schedule. Undisputed charges not paid by the applicable due date shall be subject to annual interest at the prevailing U.S. prime rate published by Citibank, N.A., from time to time or the highest rate permitted by law, whichever is lower. Customer shall also pay any collection fees, court costs, reasonable attorneys' fees, and other fees, costs, and charges incurred by Metavante in collecting payment of the charges and any other amounts for which Customer is liable under the terms and conditions of this Agreement and which shall be due thirty (30) days following the date of invoice. Customer agrees to maintain a depository account with Oriental Bank and Trust for the payment of amounts payable hereunder and hereby authorizes Metavante to initiate debit entries to such account for the payment of amounts payable hereunder. Customer agrees to provide Metavante with any and all information necessary for Metavante to initiate such debit entries via the Automated Clearing House (ACH) system.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days following the date of invoice, while the subsequent sentence in the same section retains the thirty (30) day due date for collection fees, court costs, reasonable attorneys' fees, and other fees. This creates a direct contradiction within the section itself, as some amounts are due in 15 days and others in 30, causing confusion and making enforcement ambiguous. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'. In this case, it's within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: K. "Conversion" shall mean (i) the transfer of Customer's data processing and other information technology services to Metavante's systems; (ii) completion of upgrades, enhancements and software modifications as set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) completion of all interfaces set forth in this Agreement and full integration thereof such that Customer is able to receive the Initial Services in a live operating environment.
perturbation.changed_text: K. "Conversion" shall mean (i) the transfer of Customer's data processing and other information technology services to Metavante's systems; (ii) completion of upgrades, enhancements and software modifications as set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) completion of some interfaces set forth in this Agreement and full integration thereof such that Customer is able to receive the Initial Services in a live operating environment.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction. By changing 'all interfaces' to 'some interfaces', section K of Definitions now contradicts Section 4.1.A, which requires both parties to perform such obligations as necessary for the Conversion in a timely and efficient manner to adequately meet the scheduled dates set forth therein. The change affects which Interfaces have to be converted, causing the scheduled dates to not be met, creating uncertainty about the agreed-upon deliverables and timeline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Developing of Conversion Plan. Metavante shall, in consultation with Customer, develop a detailed, customized plan for the Conversion (the "Conversion Plan"). The Conversion Plan will include (i) a description of the tasks to be performed for the Conversion; (ii) allocation of responsibility for each of such tasks; and (iii) the estimated scheduled dates on which each task is to be performed. Each party shall designate its Conversion project leader. The Conversion project leaders for each party shall regularly communicate on the progress of the Conversion, the feasibility of the Conversion Dates specified in the Conversion Plan, and such other matters which may affect the smooth transition of the Services. Neither party shall reassign or replace its Conversion project leader during the Conversion without the consent of the other party, except if such individual voluntarily resigns, is dismissed for cause, or is unable to work due to his or her death, disability or other personal reasons. Each party agrees to provide such services and to perform such obligations as are specified as its responsibility in the Conversion Plan and as necessary for it to timely and adequately meet the scheduled dates set forth therein. Each party shall cooperate fully with all reasonable requests of the other party that are necessary to effect the Conversion in a timely and efficient manner. The preliminary Conversion Plan is attached hereto as the Conversion Plan Schedule, and shall be amended as the parties mutually agree. Metavante will be responsible for the Conversion Services defined in the Standard Conversion Services Schedule included herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Conversion' to include 'some interfaces' instead of 'all interfaces.' This directly contradicts Section 4.1.A, which requires both parties to perform obligations necessary for the Conversion in a timely manner to meet the scheduled dates. By changing 'all' to 'some,' it introduces uncertainty about which interfaces are included in the conversion, making it impossible to determine if the scheduled dates can be met and creating a direct conflict with the obligation in section A to meet those dates. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated by Customer pursuant to Section 8.2, Metavante will provide Customer with one (1) set of Data Tapes without charge.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated by Customer pursuant to Section 8.2, Metavante will not provide Customer with one (1) set of Data Tapes without charge.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 2.2. The original text states that if the Customer terminates the agreement under Section 8.2, Metavante will provide one set of data tapes without charge. The changed text states that if Customer terminates the agreement under Section 8.2, Metavante will not provide one set of data tapes without charge. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding Metavante's obligation to provide data tapes to Customer upon termination, which affects the Termination Assistance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following the expiration or early termination of this Agreement, Metavante shall provide to Customer the Customer Data in the format in which it exists on Metavante's systems (the "Data Tapes"), in accordance with Metavante's then-current standard prices for the delivery media. In addition, Metavante agrees to provide to Customer, at Customer's expense, all necessary assistance to facilitate the orderly transition of Services to Customer or its designee ("Termination Assistance"). As part of the Termination Assistance, Metavante shall assist Customer to develop a plan for the transition of all Services then being performed by Metavante under this Agreement, from Metavante to Customer or Customer's designee, on a reasonable schedule developed jointly by Metavante and Customer. Prior to providing any Termination Assistance, Metavante shall deliver to Customer a good-faith estimate of all such Expenses and charges, including charges for custom programming services. Customer understands and agrees that all Expenses and charges for Termination Assistance shall be computed in accordance with Metavante's then-current standard prices for such products, materials, and services. Customer shall pay for the Customer Data and any Termination Assistance in advance of Metavante providing such data or assistance. Nothing contained herein shall obligate Customer to receive Termination Assistance from Metavante. In the event this Agreement is terminated by Customer pursuant to Section 8.2, Metavante will not provide Customer with one (1) set of Data Tapes without charge.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Metavante will not provide a free set of data tapes upon termination under Section 8.2, while the contradicted text, which includes the changed text, implies that under normal circumstances, data tapes would be provided at Metavante's standard prices, but contradicts itself by stating that under Section 8.2 termination, they are not provided without charge. The original text clearly states they would be provided free of charge. This discrepancy creates a direct contradiction regarding Metavante's obligation, making the terms of the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.
